storageManager.title: 💾 Storage Management
storageManager.whyTitle: Why is there insufficient storage space?
storageManager.reason1: Browser local storage (localStorage) is usually limited to 5-10MB
storageManager.reason2: IndexedDB, although larger, also has limits (usually 50% of disk space)
storageManager.reason3: Large files (videos, images, documents) quickly consume storage space
storageManager.reason4: Old data is not automatically cleaned up and continues to accumulate
storageManager.cleanOldDataDesc: Automatically delete data older than 30 days to free up storage space
storageManager.clearAllDesc: Delete all locally stored data (configuration will be retained)
storageManager.useServerDesc: Start the API server to store data on the server instead of in the browser
storageManager.suggest1: Regularly clean old data. It is recommended to do so once a month
storageManager.suggest2: For large files (>10MB), it is recommended to use server storage
storageManager.suggest3: If you use it frequently, it is recommended to start the API server for a better storage experience
storageManager.suggest4: For video files, it is recommended to use online links instead of uploading files
storageManager.confirmCleanMsg: Are you sure you want to clean up old data older than 30 days?
storageManager.confirmClearMsg: Final confirmation: Are you really sure you want to clear all data?
storageManager.retainInfo: Configuration information (server address, etc.) will be retained.

serverConfig.title: 🌐 Server Address Configuration
serverConfig.desc: In order for other devices (phones, tablets, etc.) to scan the QR code and access it, you need to configure the server address.
serverConfig.tip:
💡 Tip:
• Development environment: Use a local network IP, such as http://192.168.1.100:5173
• Production environment: Use a public domain name, such as https://your-domain.com
• Make sure the server is running and allows external access
serverConfig.howToGetTitle: How to get the local network IP?
serverConfig.windowsTip: Windows: Open Command Prompt, enter ipconfig, and find 'IPv4 Address'
serverConfig.macTip: Mac/Linux: Open Terminal, enter ifconfig or ip addr
serverConfig.serverTip: Make sure the server listens on all interfaces: Use the --host parameter when running npm run dev
serverConfig.invalidUrl: Invalid URL format. Please use a complete address, such as http://192.168.1.100:5173
serverConfig.saveFail: Saving failed. Please check the URL format
serverConfig.confirmClear: Are you sure you want to clear the manually configured server address?

api.fileTooLarge: File is too large ({size}MB), exceeding the server limit (100MB)
api.cannotConnectApiServer:
Unable to connect to the API server ({url})

Please follow these steps to start the API server:
1. Open Terminal/Command Line
2. Go to the api-server directory
3. Run: npm start
   or double-click: start.bat (Windows)
   or run: start.ps1 (PowerShell)

After the server starts, it will run at http://localhost:3000

Note: Even if the server is unavailable, the file will still be saved to the local browser storage.
api.contentNotExistDeleted: Content does not exist, treated as deleted

serverConfigExtra.localhostWarning: Currently using localhost, which other devices cannot access. Please configure a local network IP or public address.

advancedEditorExtra.storageInsufficientContent: Insufficient storage space to save content. Please clear your browser cache or reduce the content size (e.g., remove large video files).
advancedEditorExtra.saveToLocalFailed:
⚠️ Saving to local storage failed: {msg}

Suggestions:
1. Clear browser cache
2. Reduce content size (remove large video files)
3. Start the API server to use server storage

Note: Further failures will not be prompted. Please check the console logs.

qrGeneratorExtra.confirmSaveLargeFile:
File is quite large ({size}), and may not be able to be stored in the browser!

Suggestions:
1. Use the 'online link' mode (recommended)
2. Choose a smaller file (<10MB)
3. Upload the file to the cloud and use a link

Do you still want to proceed?
qrGeneratorExtra.confirmSaveToLocal:
⚠️ File cannot be saved to the server!

Error: {msg}

If saved to local storage, only the current device can access it, and other devices will not be able to scan it.

Do you still want to save it to local storage?

Suggestions:
1. Check if the API server is running on port 3000
2. Check if the server address is configured correctly
3. Check if the network connection is normal
4. View the browser console for detailed error messages
qrGeneratorExtra.mediaStoreFailed:
Audio and video files cannot be stored!

Reason: {msg}

Suggestions:
1. Use the 'online link' mode (recommended)
2. Choose a smaller file (<50MB)
3. Upload the file to the cloud and use a link
4. Check if the API server is running normally on port 3000
5. Check if the server address is configured correctly
